title: ALL CAPS: THE HISTORY OF THE AMERICAN BASEBALL HAT
chaptersLine: Forty-plus chapters drafted, plus an introduction and a closing coda.
spanLine: From before the Civil War to the drops of 2026.
thisWeek: Banked the ninth interview of the project and folded it into the permanent archive, a designer whose studio has spent two decades renaming minor league teams.
Added a new reported section on a hat shop that has held the same address on one East Los Angeles boulevard for twenty years.
A fact check pass on the auction market for pre-1950 game worn caps sent one number in the draft a long way north.
chapterHighlights: Two brothers working a souvenir stand outside a Boston ballpark, and what they built from it.
The Tokyo collectors who took the American fitted more seriously than America did.
A Tucson ballpark, a box of old wool, and a man who refused to make caps the modern way.
The auction where six panels of wool sold for the price of a house.
